# --output-multipart-max-memory sets the ParseMultipartForm maxMemory.
# A request larger than the configured limit should not crash the handler;
# the handler can still process it (overflow goes to disk via temp files),
# so this test asserts the literal in the generated code reflects the flag.

muxt generate --use-receiver-type=T --output-multipart-max-memory=1024
muxt check

grep 'ParseMultipartForm\(1024\)' template_routes.go

exec go test -cover

-- template.gohtml --
{{define "POST /upload 201 Upload(multipart)" }}<p>{{.Result}}</p>{{end}}

-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.22
-- template.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
	"mime/multipart"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type UploadForm struct {
	File *multipart.FileHeader `name:"file"`
}

type T struct{}

func (T) Upload(form UploadForm) int {
	if form.File == nil {
		return 0
	}
	return 1
}
-- template_test.go --
package server

import (
	"bytes"
	"mime/multipart"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

func Test_under_limit(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, T{})

	body := &bytes.Buffer{}
	w := multipart.NewWriter(body)
	fw, err := w.CreateFormFile("file", "small.txt")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if _, err := fw.Write([]byte("ok")); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	if err := w.Close(); err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/upload", body)
	req.Header.Set("content-type", w.FormDataContentType())
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if rec.Result().StatusCode != http.StatusCreated {
		t.Errorf("status = %d, want %d", rec.Result().StatusCode, http.StatusCreated)
	}
}
